title: Job Description Decomposition and Evidence Mapping · Claim Audit: Outcome Extraction
summary: A listing combines recruiter language, team outcomes, essential work, preferences, copied requirements, benefits, legal text, and ambiguous signals. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the outcome extraction increment in role requirement and evidence matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.
Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Outcome Extraction · Theory Outcome Extraction
steps.instruction:
theory case — A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Outcome Extraction contract for the supplied data.

For Predict Outcome Extraction · Theory Outcome Extraction, keep Outcome Extraction connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The worked example variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording. A defensible solution applies Outcome Extraction, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to role requirement and evidence matrix.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-5
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Matching nouns matters more than understanding expected work. This fails the Outcome Extraction contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: NACE Career Readiness Competencies (Current eight-competency framework reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls observable evidence for career and self-development, communication, critical thinking, equity and inclusion, leadership, professionalism, teamwork, and technology.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.
